title: terminalia micans
intro: Terminalia micans, also known as Black Pui or Pui, is a deciduous tree species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as skin problems and digestive disorders. Terminalia micans can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as used in Ayurvedic and Unani traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
